rulings.Case Title: PETER SANDOR GARDOS VS. BANK OF AMERICA N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 26, 2013, line 5. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the first cause of action for promissory estoppel is sustained without leave to amend, moot as the only remedy sought. FAC 21. , demurrer to causes of action for fraudulent concealment (2nd ), constructive fraud (3rd), negligence (5th), NIED (6th) and UCL (4th) violation is overruled to the extent it alleges misrepresentation regarding postponement of foreclosure sale date. (To the extent these causes of action are based on failure to modify/mishandling of modification, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Aspiras v. Wells Fargo Bank (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 948). Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action fro IIED. See Price v. Wells Fargo (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 465 and Davenport v. Litton Loan Servicing (N.D. Cal. 2010) 725 F.Supp.2d 862. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action for loss of credit expectancy (8th), to set aside trustee's sale (9th) and injunction (10th). No opposition filed. = (501/HMD)
